input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule V.2.2 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.2.2
            TITLE: visibility

            CONTENT:
            rule V.2.2: Visibility
V.2.2 Visibility 
a. The driver must have sufficient visibility to the front and sides of the vehicle 
b. When seated in a normal driving position, the driver must have a minimum field of vision 
of 100° to both sides 
c. If mirrors are required for this rule, they must remain in place and adjusted to enable the 
required visibility throughout all dynamic events.

final_answer: V.2.2 Visibility a. The driver must have sufficient visibility to the front and sides of the vehicle b. When seated in a normal driving position, the driver must have a minimum field of vision of 100° to both sides c. If mirrors are required for this rule, they must remain in place and adjusted to enable the required visibility throughout all dynamic events.
